mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a weakening demand for residential construction that reduces economic growth and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST < 1.2M are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production above 102 signals a strengthening supply chain that increases corporate profitability and reduces inflation expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 102 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in real disposable personal income above 2.5% signals a strengthening consumer spending that increases economic growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial jobless claims below 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200K are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the consumer price index above 3.5% signals a strengthening inflation that reduces purchasing power and increases interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 3.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in real GDP below 2.2% signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 2.2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
